author: taulepton_
content: Hey <@592004585506340885> in the consensus specs https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/_features/eip8025/fork.md I notice we have defined a fork for EIP 8025. In practice we do not want there to be a hard fork as this is an optional feature. I assume this has been introduced to support testing in the consensus specs?

author: taulepton_
content: <@427491045308235776> just an FYI regarding p2p spec PR, myself and <@531934490826768418> had discussed the migration from Metadata and concluded it was preferable to have ExecutionProofStatus instead. This is to prevent any potential for future conflict with modifications to Metadata / GetMetada due to core protocol changes in the future.

author: kevaundray
content: Ah okay, I pinged Manu on the PR re this (glad this is resolved)

author: kevaundray
content:
PR looks good from high level:

- Add ExecutionProofsByRange (nothing suprising here since we have same for blobs for range syncing)

- Remove metadata changes and revert back to v3. Instead we now have ExecutionProofStatus to tell us when a peer is a zk attester and also what their latest proven slot is

author: kevaundray
content:
> ExecutionProofsByRange
Would expect that this looks very similar to the blobs version

author: taulepton_
content: Agreed, relatively simple additions to the p2p protocol that follow standards from other RPCs.

author: taulepton_
content:
<@427491045308235776> how are you thinking about the announce + request pattern for proofs that we discussed previously with Raul and others from the p2p team. 

I think that we should probably leave that out of scope for now and reconsider it once the new p2p implementation lands. It may be that we want to do something different such as gossiping shares of proofs as is planned for execution payloads. Either way I think it may be a bit soon to optimise and we probably want to freeze a v1 of the spec asap.

author: taulepton_
content: hmmm yep I think so. Something about chunking blocks and gossiping chunks 😅

author: kevaundray
content: yep would generally agree -- I think we should write down the announce + request pattern, perhaps in an issue or something so that we can come back to it with fresh eyes after ethp2p has landed

author: taulepton_
content: Sounds good. what repo should I capture that in?

author: kevaundray
content: consensus specs would be great ser
